topics.label: Cluster Setup
topics.description: Securing the Kubernetes cluster foundation: applying CIS benchmarks, enforcing network policies, hardening the API server, configuring Ingress TLS, and protecting node metadata endpoints.

examScenarios.title: Apply CIS Benchmark Controls
examScenarios.prompt: Given a kubeadm-provisioned cluster, identify API server flags that violate CIS Level 1 controls and correct them.
examScenarios.focus: Knowing which kube-apiserver flags correspond to CIS controls and how to audit them with kube-bench.

examScenarios.title: Restrict Pod Egress with NetworkPolicy
examScenarios.prompt: Create a NetworkPolicy that denies all egress from Pods in the 'payments' namespace except to port 5432 on Pods labeled db=postgres.
examScenarios.focus: NetworkPolicy spec syntax, podSelector, namespaceSelector, egress rules, and ports.

topics.label: Cluster Hardening
topics.description: Reducing the Kubernetes attack surface through RBAC least-privilege, ServiceAccount hygiene, API server access restrictions, and timely upgrades.

studyPointers.label: Controlling Access to the API Server

examScenarios.title: Audit and Tighten RBAC
examScenarios.prompt: A Role in the 'dev' namespace grants 'get,list,watch,create,update,patch,delete' on all resources. Reduce it to the minimum needed for a read-only CI job.
examScenarios.focus: Role vs ClusterRole, verbs, resourceNames, and the principle of least privilege.

examScenarios.title: Disable Automounting of ServiceAccount Tokens
examScenarios.prompt: Ensure Pods in the 'frontend' namespace never receive an automounted service account token unless explicitly requested.
examScenarios.focus: automountServiceAccountToken on ServiceAccount and Pod spec.

topics.label: System Hardening
topics.description: Reducing the OS-level attack surface on Kubernetes nodes through AppArmor profiles, seccomp filters, privilege restriction, and minimizing installed packages.

examScenarios.title: Enforce an AppArmor Profile on a Pod
examScenarios.prompt: A custom AppArmor profile named 'k8s-apparmor-example-deny-write' is loaded on the node. Apply it to a specific container in a Pod.
examScenarios.focus: AppArmor annotation syntax, profile loading verification, and container-level application.

examScenarios.title: Apply a Restricted seccomp Profile
examScenarios.prompt: Configure a Pod to use the RuntimeDefault seccomp profile and verify it is being enforced.
examScenarios.focus: securityContext.seccompProfile field, RuntimeDefault vs Localhost profiles.

topics.label: Minimize Microservice Vulnerabilities
topics.description: Hardening workloads using OPA/Gatekeeper admission policies, Pod Security Standards, Secrets management best practices, container sandboxing runtimes, and mutual TLS between services.

examScenarios.title: Block Privileged Containers with Gatekeeper
examScenarios.prompt: Write an OPA Gatekeeper ConstraintTemplate that denies any Pod with securityContext.privileged: true.
examScenarios.focus: ConstraintTemplate Rego logic, Constraint resource, admission webhook enforcement.

examScenarios.title: Run a Workload in gVisor
examScenarios.prompt: Deploy a Pod using the gVisor RuntimeClass (runsc) and confirm it is sandboxed.
examScenarios.focus: RuntimeClass resource, handler field, Pod runtimeClassName reference.

topics.label: Supply Chain Security
topics.description: Securing the software delivery pipeline: minimizing base images, scanning images for vulnerabilities, enforcing signed image policies, and using admission controllers like ImagePolicyWebhook.

examScenarios.title: Scan an Image with Trivy
examScenarios.prompt: Scan the image 'nginx:1.21' for HIGH and CRITICAL CVEs and output results to a file, then choose a safer alternative base image.
examScenarios.focus: trivy image command syntax, severity filtering, and image minimization strategy.

examScenarios.title: Enable ImagePolicyWebhook
examScenarios.prompt: Configure the API server to use an ImagePolicyWebhook that rejects images not from the corporate registry.
examScenarios.focus: AdmissionConfiguration file, ImagePolicyWebhook plugin, and kubeconfig for the webhook backend.

topics.label: Monitoring, Logging and Runtime Security
topics.description: Detecting and responding to threats at runtime using Falco rules, Kubernetes audit logging, behavioral monitoring, immutable container filesystems, and threat indicators.

examScenarios.title: Write a Falco Rule for Shell Spawning
examScenarios.prompt: Write a Falco rule that triggers when a shell is spawned inside any container in the 'production' namespace.
examScenarios.focus: Falco rule structure: rule, desc, condition, output, priority; syscall and k8s metadata macros.

examScenarios.title: Configure API Server Audit Policy
examScenarios.prompt: Create an audit policy that logs RequestResponse level events for Secrets and None level for everything else.
examScenarios.focus: AuditPolicy resource, levels (None, Metadata, Request, RequestResponse), rules ordering.
